title: Tabernaemontana glandulosa
intro: Tabernaemontana glandulosa, also known as the Madagascar inside-fruit tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as possessing antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Tabernaemontana glandulosa include alkaloids, particularly voacangine, voacristine, and voacamine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare various herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Tabernaemontana glandulosa is classified within the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This classification is part of the larger clade of angiosperms, which also includes other flowering plant families. The genus Tabernaemontana is a group of evergreen or deciduous trees and shrubs native to tropical regions. The species Tabernaemontana glandulosa is native to tropical Asia. It is a member of the order Gentianales, which is a group of flowering plants that includes other families such as Loganiaceae and Rubiaceae.
uses: Tabernaemontana glandulosa has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions. In some parts of the world, it is used to manage fever, headaches, and respiratory issues.
